meaning: n.高加索人,白种人adj.高加索的,白种人的
examples: The main characters were mostly Caucasian.
It is interesting to note that some studies show Caucasian American children have earlier childhood memories than Korean children do.
A large Caucasian inmate steps forward.
And there, in the corner, hiding behind a white Caucasian sheepdog, sits a lion cub.
The faces featured in it are uniformly beautiful and tanned - but overwhelmingly Caucasian.
